front: Poem: opens 'You who hear the sound, in scattered rhymes'; 366 poems addressed to Laura
Poem: 'Now I have become an old tale amongst all these people' / 'shame is the fruit of my vanities'
Poem: love found speaker 'all weaponless' on the day 'the sun's ray was darkened'
Poem: 'My ship sails on, packed with forgetfulness' — ship between Charybdis and Scylla
Author: famous letter describing climbing Mont Ventoux for pleasure
Author: read Augustine at the summit — 'men wonder at the heights of mountains but consider themselves not'
Poem: unfinished Latin epic about Scipio Africanus in the Second Punic War; contains 'Scipio's Dream'
Poem: six allegorical poems depicting successive conquering: Love, Chastity, Death, Fame, Time, Eternity
Author: 'Letter to Posterity' — hailed as first Western autobiography since Augustine's Confessions
Author: rediscovered Cicero's ad Atticum letters
Author: inspired his own Epistolae Familiares and Seniles
Author: letter to Agapito Colonna credited with coining the concept of the 'Dark Ages'
Author: Liber sine nomine (Book Without a Name) — harshly criticized the Avignon Papacy
Author: Secretum — imagined dialogues with Augustine about forgetting mortality
Poem: five stanzas each ending with a quotation from a previous poet (Arnaut Daniel, Cavalcanti, Dante, Cino da Pistoia, then himself)
Author: translated into English by Thomas Wyatt and Henry Howard, Earl of Surrey
Author: spread the sonnet form to England
